description: Insurance must be calculated on aggregated balance per depositor per ORC, not per individual account. Accounts belonging to the same depositor under the same ORC must be summed before applying SMDIA.
remediation: Aggregate balances by (depositor_id, orc_code) before applying SMDIA. Rewrite sp_calculate_insurance to use GROUP BY depositor_id, orc_type.

control_name: IRR ORC Falls Through to SGL
description: Accounts with ORC type IRR (Irrevocable Trust) are not implemented in the ORC assignment program and silently default to SGL. This understates or miscalculates insurance coverage under 12 CFR 330.13.
remediation: Add IRR branch to ORC-ASSIGNMENT.cob. Add IRR to insurance.supported.orc.types in insurance-rules.properties. Re-run ORC assignment for all IRR trust accounts.

description: EBP (Employee Benefit Plan) coverage is applied per-plan at $250K instead of per participant per 12 CFR 330.14. For plans with hundreds of participants, this massively understates insured coverage.
remediation: Change insurance-rules.properties ebp.mode to PER_PARTICIPANT. Update calculation logic to multiply participant_count by SMDIA.

description: SMDIA ($250,000) is hardcoded in insurance-rules.properties and stored procedures instead of being loaded from a configurable parameter table. Any regulatory change requires a code deployment.
remediation: Move SMDIA to a DB config table. Load at runtime. Support hot-reload without redeployment.

description: Only pending reason codes P01-P05 are defined. FDIC IT Guide requires P01-P10. Accounts needing codes P06-P10 silently default to SGL instead of routing to the pending file.
remediation: Define pending codes P06-P10 in insurance-rules.properties and in all ORC assignment code paths.

control_name: Hold Amount Must Not Reduce Allocated Balance
description: Hold amounts are informational per FDIC IT Guide and must not be subtracted from the balance used for insurance determination. Any pipeline stage that subtracts hold_amount from balance before insurance calculation is in violation.
remediation: Remove hold_amount subtraction from transformation stage. Hold amount must remain an informational-only field and must never reduce balance used for insurance calculation.

description: 12 CFR 360.8 requires insurance calculation to use close-of-business balances. The current system does not enforce a COB cutoff timestamp, potentially including intraday transactions.
remediation: Enforce a COB cutoff timestamp. Filter account balances to those as of COB on calculation_date. Reject records with last_activity_date after COB.

description: JNT accounts require all co-owners to be natural persons per 12 CFR 330.9. ORC-ASSIGNMENT.cob does not verify natural_person status for joint co-owners. A business entity could be misclassified as JNT.
remediation: Add natural_person check to ORC assignment for all JNT accounts. Verify both primary owner and joint co-owner are natural persons before assigning JNT.

control_name: IRR Trust Not Implemented - Falls Through to SGL
description: Irrevocable trust accounts (IRR) are not handled in TRUST-INSURANCE-CALC.cob and default to SGL coverage ($250K) instead of the correct per-beneficiary-interest calculation per 12 CFR 330.13.
remediation: Implement IRR calculation branch in TRUST-INSURANCE-CALC.cob. For each IRR trust, calculate coverage as SUM(per-beneficiary-interest) with SMDIA per non-contingent interest.

description: TRUST-INSURANCE-CALC.cob only counts PRIMARY beneficiaries for REV trust coverage. CONTINGENT and REMAINDER beneficiaries are excluded, understating coverage per 12 CFR 330.10.
remediation: Count all unique non-contingent beneficiaries (PRIMARY + REMAINDER) for REV trust coverage. Verify beneficiary data completeness before calculation.

description: EBP trust coverage uses plan-level participant_count instead of actual vested participants per 12 CFR 330.14. For TR-004 (1247 participants, $45M), coverage is understated by hundreds of millions.
remediation: Use per-participant vested balance for EBP coverage. Obtain actual vested participant counts from benefit plan administrator. Multiply by SMDIA for max insured.

description: TRUST-INSURANCE-CALC.cob counts deceased beneficiaries in coverage calculation, inflating insured amount. Each deceased beneficiary adds $250K to the reported insured total incorrectly.
remediation: Filter out deceased beneficiaries before calculating coverage. Update beneficiary records with death dates. Apply 12 CFR 330.10 grace period rules for recently deceased.

control_name: Trust Not Aggregated Across Same Grantor/Beneficiary
description: Coverage is calculated per-trust rather than aggregated across all trusts with the same grantor and overlapping beneficiaries. This allows bypassing SMDIA limits through multiple trust accounts.
remediation: Aggregate trust coverage by grantor across all trusts with overlapping beneficiaries before applying SMDIA. Implement beneficiary-level linkage across trusts.

control_name: Custodial IRA Defaults to SGL Instead of CRA
description: Type CUS (custodial) accounts default to SGL ORC instead of the underlying CRA classification. IRA custodial accounts (TR-007) should use CRA for separate $250K coverage.
remediation: Map CUS trust_type with IRA product codes to CRA ORC. Add explicit custodial-to-ORC mapping rules in ORC assignment logic.

control_name: OFAC Screening Batch Mode Only - 6 Hour Lag
description: ofac_screening.py runs in batch mode with up to 6-hour delay. Real-time wires are processed without current OFAC clearance. Wires WT20260301003 and WT20260301012 have NOT_SCREENED status.
remediation: Move OFAC screening to synchronous pre-settlement step. Block wire settlement until OFAC clearance is obtained. For real-time channels, integrate with OFAC API.

control_name: Unscreened Wire Included in Insurance Calculation
description: Wire transactions with ofac_status=NOT_SCREENED are included in FDIC insurance calculation. Funds that may be subject to OFAC blocking should not be counted toward insured deposits.
remediation: Exclude all wire transactions with ofac_status != CLEARED from the insurance calculation basis. Create a separate suspense bucket for unscreened amounts.

control_name: SDN List Updated Weekly Instead of Daily
description: The SDN list in ofac_screening.py is updated weekly from the OFAC website. The minimum required update frequency for sanctions compliance is daily. This creates a 7-day window of potential false-clears.
remediation: Update SDN list download to daily schedule. Integrate with OFAC API for real-time updates. Add hash verification of downloaded SDN file.

control_name: Wire Insurance Not Aggregated Across All Channels
description: sp_calculate_wire_insurance.sql calculates insurance per wire customer without cross-system aggregation. A depositor's wire balance + deposit account balance + trust balance must all be aggregated before applying SMDIA.
remediation: Implement cross-system aggregation join: wire_customers + deposit_accounts + trust_accounts grouped by CIF/party_id and orc_type before applying SMDIA.

control_name: ACH Returns Not Deducted from Insurance Basis
description: ACH RETURNED transactions (status=RETURNED) are not netted against original ACH credits. WT20260301006 ($180K NSF return) is not deducted from the insurance basis for WC-002, overstating insured deposits.
remediation: Net ACH RETURNED transactions against ACH credits by customer ID before calculating insurance basis. Track returns as negative adjustments in the reconciliation stage.

description: MT202 (bank-to-bank) wire WT20260301012 has an empty ordering_customer_id. Without a CIF linkage, this wire cannot be attributed to a depositor for ORC assignment or insurance calculation.
remediation: For MT202 messages, look up ordering institution BIC in correspondent bank registry. Assign GOV or BUS ORC based on institution type. Route to pending if unresolvable.

description: GOV1/GOV2/GOV3 deposits require pledged collateral for uninsured portions per 12 CFR 330.15. Accounts ACCT-018 (GOV2, $15M) and ACCT-023 (GOV1, $8.5M) have collateral_pledged=FALSE with no verification.
remediation: Add collateral verification step for all GOV ORC accounts. Require collateral registry linkage for uninsured portions. Alert if collateral < uninsured_amount.
